changes: Added terminal_width: u16 and terminal_height: u16 fields to App struct
Implemented on_resize(width, height) method to store new dimensions
Implemented visible_column_indices() to compute layout-safe columns with priority-based hiding (merged → done)
Added required_width() helper function to calculate minimum width for visible columns
Initial terminal size queried at startup with crossterm::terminal::size() fallback to 80×24
Added 8 comprehensive unit tests covering resize scenarios, minimal height detection, and column visibility logic
Updated test helper app instantiation to initialize terminal_width: 120, terminal_height: 40
Updated AppEvent::Resize(w, h) handler to call app.on_resize(w, h)
Added frame area dimensions sync to App state in draw() function
Implemented render_minimal_status() function for height < 10 constraint
Minimal status displays: 'bop · P:N R:N D:N F:N M:N · {mode}' as single-line fallback
Updated draw() docstring to document minimal mode behavior
render_kanban() now calls app.visible_column_indices() to filter columns before layout
Early return if visible columns list is empty
Layout constraints updated to use only visible columns
Updated function docstring to document column hiding behavior

patterns_discovered.description: Terminal width/height stored both in App state and synced from frame area at render time. Ensures consistency across state machine and UI rendering.
Full kanban layout for height ≥ 10; minimal single-line status bar for height < 10. Maintains usability in extremely constrained terminal sizes (split panes, embedded terminals).
When terminal width insufficient, columns hidden in fixed priority order: merged (lowest), then done, keeping essential pending/running/failed visible longest.
Layout recomputed at render time (visible_column_indices) rather than on every resize event. Ratatui cell-level diff handles incremental screen updates.
MIN_COL_WIDTH (15), COLLAPSED_COL_WIDTH (3), and HIDE_PRIORITY array defined as function-scoped consts. Controls column width requirements and hiding order.

gotchas_discovered.issue: Initial terminal size unavailable at startup
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: crossterm::terminal::size() queried with fallback to 80×24 default when unavailable (e.g. non-TTY contexts)

gotchas_discovered.impact: Collapsed columns (3 chars) require far less width than expanded (15 chars). Required_width() must differentiate to prevent premature column hiding

gotchas_discovered.issue: Panic on zero-height or zero-width render area
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: render_minimal_status() includes early return guard: `if area.height == 0 || area.width == 0 { return; }`

gotchas_discovered.issue: Frame area dimensions vs App state divergence
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: draw() syncs app.terminal_width/height from frame.area() on every render to keep in sync

gotchas_discovered.issue: Empty visible columns vector edge case
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: render_kanban() early-returns if visible.is_empty() to prevent layout panic

discoveries.approach_details: Resize handling fully implemented across event loop, state management, and rendering pipeline. Terminal dimensions tracked in App state, retrieved from crossterm at startup, updated via AppEvent::Resize, and synced from frame area at render time. Layout dynamically hides low-priority columns (merged → done) when width insufficient. Minimal single-line status bar (P:N R:N D:N F:N M:N format) renders for height < 10. Full kanban board uses visible_column_indices() to filter columns before rendering. Comprehensive test suite (8 tests) covers edge cases: minimal height boundary (9 vs 10), wide terminal (all columns visible), narrow terminal (merged hidden first, then done), and width calculation correctness.

recommendations.suggestion: Extract MIN_COL_WIDTH and COLLAPSED_COL_WIDTH to app-level config or environment variables to allow users to tune layout constraints without recompilation
Profile visible_column_indices() on very large terminal widths (e.g. 500+ columns) to verify no O(n²) behavior in loop; consider memoization if resize frequency is high
Add animated column transitions (fade-in/fade-out or slide) when columns are hidden/revealed due to resize, to provide visual feedback of layout change
Document minimal status bar format (`bop · P:N R:N D:N F:N M:N · {mode}`) in user guide and help text for users encountering very small terminals
Add telemetry or debug logging for resize event frequency in constrained environments (e.g. split panes) to detect pathological resize storms
